rationale: The postcondition requires concrete HashMap equality (post1_self_.send_state.epmap == post2_self_.send_state.epmap and the same for receive_state.epmap). HashMap<V> wraps std::collections::HashMap and only exposes an uninterp spec view, so two HashMaps with the same Map view can be unequal concretely. The ensures of send_single_cmessage (which is #[verifier::external_body]) only constrains the abstract view post@ via SingleDelivery::send_single_message; it does not constrain post.send_state.epmap (the concrete HashMap). Hence concrete epmap equality is not derivable. Verified empirically: even `assert(post1.send_state.epmap == pre.send_state.epmap)` is rejected by Verus when the spec forces post@ == pre@ at the abstract level. The previous attempt_01 proof block (extensional view-equality chain via #[verifier::ext_equal]) also fails for the same reason.
